pattern: Whether you can finish every milestone reduces to comparing the largest project against the sum of all the others, like interleaving the most frequent item.
hints: The only thing that can force you to stop early is one project being so large you run out of other work to interleave.
Let max be the biggest milestone count and rest be the sum of the others.
If max <= rest + 1 you can finish everything; otherwise you can only do rest other milestones plus rest+1 of the big project, giving 2*rest + 1.

edge_inputs.description: one dominant project leaves a milestone unfinished
single large project is capped at one milestone
max equals rest plus one boundary, everything finishes

stress.description: large arrays with occasional dominating project
stress.note: mix of finishable and dominated cases
